titel: The Commission plans to issue EU bonds with a value of 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026.
html:
European Commission Press Release, Brussels, June 24, 2026.

The European Commission has announced its intention to issue up to €80 billion in EU bonds during the second half of 2026. This means that the total planned issuance of EU bonds for 2026 will be €180 billion, which is consistent with the Commission's previous indications for the year.
tldr: The EU plans to borrow 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026. This is part of a larger plan to borrow a total of 180 billion euros throughout the entire year.
lattlast:
The European Commission, which serves as the government of the EU, has announced its intention to borrow money by issuing so-called EU bonds. A bond is a form of loan issued by the EU to raise funds for its operations.

During the second half of 2026, they plan to borrow up to 80 billion euros. This is part of a larger plan for the entire year 2026.

Taking into account all the planned borrowings for the entire year, the total amount will be 180 billion euros. This aligns with what the EU has previously stated it needs to borrow during 2026.
